page: Amazon Web Services r7i.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It has no local storage or GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mix of average and weak results. The instance achieves average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2008.49 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1829.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, as seen in the Geekbench multi-core score of 4038.0 and stress-ng multi-core score of 4002.86. Memory bandwidth is also weak, measuring 24.76 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Conversely, single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench text processing show strong performance, scoring 30.35 GB/sec and 2360.0 respectively.

The r7i.xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high multi-core compute density or local storage. Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for applications needing substantial memory capacity without paying for excess CPU cores. Suitable workloads include caching layers, in-memory databases, text processing, and lightweight language model inference, where its average prompt processing speeds of 32.19 tokens/sec for 7B models are sufficient.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per core. It includes no local storage or GPU resources, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance alongside weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results, though single-threaded gzip compression and text processing perform strongly. Qualitatively, the r7i.xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy applications that do not demand high multi-core compute density. It is best suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Strong single-threaded compression and text processing performance
Well-suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
